سطح مرتفع بلوچستان کوہ سلیمان اور کھیر تھر پہاڑی سلسلوں کے مغرب میں واقع ہے۔

یہ خطہ پاکستان کے جنوب مغربی حصے میں پھیلا ہوا ہے۔

questions.title: پاکستان اور ازبکستان کے درمیان پہلا فضائی معاہدہ کہاں ہوا؟
questions.explanation:
پاکستان اور ازبکستان کے درمیان پہلا فضائی معاہدہ 1993 میں اسلام آباد میں ہوا۔

اس معاہدے کا مقصد دونوں ممالک کے درمیان فضائی روابط کو فروغ دینا تھا۔

questions.title: The _______ was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders led by the Aga Khan III at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.
questions.explanation:
In 1906, Sir Aga Khan met with a prominent Congress leader at the Viceregal Lodge in Shimla


The Simla Deputation was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders


The Aga Khan III led them at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.


The deputation aimed to convince Lord Minto, then Viceroy of India, to grant Muslims greater representation in politics.

questions.title: انگریز تاجروں کے تجارتی ادارے کا نام کیا تھا؟
questions.explanation:
ایسٹ انڈیا کمپنی 1600 میں انگلینڈ میں قائم کی گئی تھی تاکہ ہندوستان اور ایشیا کے دیگر حصوں میں تجارت کی جا سکے۔

یہ کمپنی بعد میں برصغیر میں برطانوی حکمرانی کی بنیاد بنی۔

questions.title: A Hindu temple was attacked by the mob in district _____ in December 2020.
questions.explanation:
In December 2020, a Hindu temple was attacked and set on fire by a mob in Karak district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

The attack was condemned by Pakistani authorities, and the Supreme Court ordered its reconstruction.

questions.title: کو ہندوستان میں مغربی تعلیم کا بانی تصور کیا جاتا ہے۔ ______
questions.explanation:
لارڈ میکالے کو ہندوستان میں مغربی تعلیم کا بانی تصور کیا جاتا ہے۔
اس نے میکالے منٹس 1835 میں انگریزی تعلیم کو فروغ دینے کی حمایت کی، جس سے برطانوی تعلیمی نظام متعارف ہوا۔

questions.title: مغربی پاکستان کے چاروں صوبوں اور ریاستوں کو یکجا کرکے 14 اکتوبر ______ کو ون یونٹ قرار دیا گیا۔
questions.explanation:
چودہ اکتوبر 1955 کو مغربی پاکستان کے تمام صوبوں اور ریاستوں کو ون یونٹ میں یکجا کیا گیا۔

اس کا مقصد انتظامی سادگی اور مشرقی و مغربی پاکستان میں توازن قائم کرنا تھا۔

questions.title: Due to the good treatment ______ of Mughals, the Rajputs had become loyal, but abusing these favors, the Rajputs got stronger.
questions.explanation:
Emperor Akbar adopted a policy of religious tolerance and granted high ranks to Rajputs in his administration.

This loyalty helped stabilize the Mughal Empire, but later, some Rajputs asserted independence.

questions.title: Who got the vote of confidence from the National Assembly on May 27, 1993?
questions.explanation:
On May 27, 1993, Nawaz Sharif received a vote of confidence from the National Assembly during his first tenure as Prime Minister (1990–1993).

This occurred amid political tensions with President Ghulam Ishaq Khan, which eventually led to both leaders resigning in July 1993 as part of an agreement mediated by the military.

Nawaz Sharif became the 12th Prime Minister of Pakistan on November 6, 1990, succeeding Benazir Bhutto.

questions.title: When the Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan (IDBP) was established?
questions.explanation:
The Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan (IDBP) was established in 1961 to promote industrial growth.

It provided financial assistance to small and medium-sized industries in Pakistan.

Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan headquarters: Karachi

questions.title: After independence, in 1947, the Pakhtoonistan stunt was raised in Khyber P.K. by?
questions.explanation: After independence, in 1947, the Pakhtoonistan stunt was raised in Khyber P.K. by Khan Abdul

questions.title: Rock salt range of the ________ famous all over the world.
questions.explanation:
The Salt Range is located in Punjab and contains famous salt mines like Khewra, Kalabagh, and Warcha.

It also has notable lakes such as Namal Lake, Khabikki Lake, and Uchhali Lake.

Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest peak in the Salt Range.

Tilla Jogian (Jhelum District) is the second-highest point in the region.

questions.title: ہمارا قومی ترانہ کونسی ہیت میں لکھی گئی ہے؟
questions.explanation:
مخمس وہ شعری ہیت ہے جس میں ہر بند میں پانچ مصرعے ہوتے ہیں۔
پاکستان کا قومی ترانہ حفیظ جالندھری نے مخمس ہیت میں لکھا تھا۔
پورا قومی ترانہ بجنے میں ایک منٹ بیس سیکنڈ لگتے ہیں۔
پاک قومی ترانے میں کل 15 مصرعے ہیں۔

questions.title: Shah Jahan was the king of how many provinces?
questions.explanation:
During Shah Jahan's reign, the Mughal Empire was divided into 22 provinces for administrative control.

These provinces were established to improve governance and tax collection across the empire.

Shah Jahan's real name was Shahab-ud-Din Muhammad Khurram.

He built the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife, Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.title: درج ذیل میں سے شہید ملت کس شخصیت کو کہا جاتا ہے؟
questions.explanation:
لیاقت علی خان پاکستان کے پہلے وزیرِاعظم تھے، جنہیں 16 اکتوبر 1951 کو راولپنڈی میں شہید کر دیا گیا۔

ان کی شہادت کے باعث انہیں "شہیدِ ملت" کا لقب دیا گیا۔

questions.title: At the time of the independence of Pakistan, who was the Chief of Pakistan Air Force?
questions.explanation:
At the time of Pakistan's independence, the Chief of the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) was Air Vice Marshal (later Air Marshal) M. Asghar Khan. 

From: 23 July 1957- 22 July 1965

questions.title: After independence who was the Chief of Pakistan’s Naval Forces?
questions.explanation:
Rear Admiral James Wilfred Jefford was appointed the first Commander of the Royal Pakistan Navy.

He served as the Commander-in-Chief of the Royal Pakistan Navy until 1953. 

He was a Royal Navy officer.

questions.title: The All India Muslim League’s meeting was held in Delhi on 20th March 1927, under the leadership of Quaid-e-Azam. He presented a set of proposals called ______?
questions.explanation:
Delhi Proposals 1927 (Key Points)

Reasons:

  • Congress promoted Hindu interests and ignored Muslim demands.
  • Hindu Mahasabha (1923) threatened Muslim rights.
  • Need to protect Muslim political interests and discuss separate electorates.

Demands:

  • 1/3 seats for Muslims in the Central Legislature.
  • Sindh to be separated from Bombay and given provincial status.
  • British reforms to be extended to Baluchistan & NWFP.
  • Muslim representation in Punjab & Bengal to be based on population.
  • Jinnah agreed to drop demand for separate electorates if these demands were met.

questions.title: From where the skill of handicraft is inherited to Pakistani people?
questions.explanation:
Handicraft means دستکاری

The skill of handicrafts in Pakistan dates back to ancient civilizations like the Indus Valley Civilization (2500 BCE), where artisans crafted pottery, textiles, and jewelry.

Traditional crafts such as embroidery, pottery, and woodwork have been passed down through generations, preserving the cultural heritage.

questions.title: Civil Disobedience and non-cooperation movement were carried out jointly under the supervision of ______ and ______ in 1920.
questions.explanation:
The Non-Cooperation Movement started in 1920.

The leader of the Non-cooperation Movement was Mahatma Gandhia and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

The non-cooperation movement was a mass movement that involved participation from the nationalists as well as the public. 

After a series of events including the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1920.

The Non-cooperation Movement was called off by Mahatma Gandhi after the Chauri Chaura incident in February 1922.

questions.title: In which year Maharaja Ranjit Singh was crowned?
questions.explanation:
Maharaja Ranjit Singh was crowned as the ruler of the Sikh Empire in 1801 at the age of 20.
He established a strong and secular rule, uniting various Sikh misls under one empire.

questions.title: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto appointed Mumtaz Ali Bhutto as Governor of which province of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
Mumtaz Ali Bhutto was appointed as the Governor of Sindh by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto in 1971.

He later served as the Chief Minister of Sindh in 1972.

questions.title: The report which was presented by Nehru for the future Constitution of India was rejected by ______?
questions.explanation:
• The Nehru Report (1928) proposed a united India without separate electorates, which was unacceptable to the Muslim League.

  • Jinnah rejected it and later presented his Fourteen Points (1929) in response.

questions.title: The Reshmi Romal Movement's headquarter was in ______?
questions.explanation:
Maulana Mahmood ul Hassan (1858-1920) was a Indian Muslim scholar and independence activist who founded the Tahrik-e-Reshmi Rumal (Silken Handkerchief Movement) in 1915.

Its headquarters was in Kabul, where efforts were made to seek Afghan and Ottoman support against British rule in India.

questions.title: In the mid nineteenth century, a famous political thinker of Afghanistan Syed Jamal-ud-Din started a movement of unity and harmony among the Muslims, known as ____?
questions.explanation:
Syed Jamal-ud-Din Afghani started the Islamic Movement, also known as Pan-Islamism, in the mid-19th century.

His goal was to unite Muslims globally against Western imperialism and promote Islamic revival.

questions.title: Exhibition Ideas 2000 was organized in Karachi from November 14th to 17th 2000 for ______?
questions.explanation:
Exhibition Ideas 2000 was organized in Karachi to promote economic activities, showcasing trade, industry, and investment opportunities.

It aimed to boost business collaboration and highlight Pakistan's economic potential.

questions.title: The tenure of Wafaqi Mohtasib is ______ years.
questions.explanation:
The tenure of Wafaqi Mohtasib is 4 years.

Wafaqi Mohtasib is a government department formulated via presidential order I of 1983, by President General Zia ul Haq.

questions.title: How much Afghans come to Pakistan after 1979 war?
questions.explanation:
After the 1979 Soviet invasion, around 4.5 million Afghans took refuge in Pakistan, making it one of the largest refugee movements.

The influx affected Pakistan’s economy, security, and demographics, with many refugees settling in camps and urban areas.

questions.title: Diplomatic ties between Pakistan and _____ were established in 1952.
questions.explanation:
Pakistan and Japan established diplomatic ties in 1952, shortly after Japan’s post-war recovery.

Both countries have maintained strong economic and trade relations, with Japan being a key investor in Pakistan.

questions.title: At what age Ashoka became a king?
questions.answer: When he was only 20 years old.
questions.explanation:
Ashoka became the Mauryan emperor in 268 BCE at the age of 20 after the death of his father, Emperor Bindusara.

He later became famous for spreading Buddhism and his Dhamma policy after the Kalinga War.

questions.title: Which of the following is one of the largest donors of aid to Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
Saudi Arabia has been one of the largest donors of financial aid and support to Pakistan, providing loans, grants, and oil assistance.

Other major donors include China and international organizations like the IMF and World Bank, but Saudi Arabia has consistently provided substantial aid.

questions.title: When Muhammad Bin Qasim attacked Sindh, the strength of the army at that time was ________ thousand soldiers.
questions.explanation: Muhammad ibn Qasim led the Muslim conquest of Sindh from Raja Dahir in the battle of Aror in 712 AD.

questions.title: When did Mahmood Ghaznavi's successful attack on Somnath occur?
questions.explanation:
Mahmood Ghaznavi successfully attacked the Somnath temple in 1025 A.D., looting its wealth and demolishing the structure.

Mahmud of Ghazni: Turkish invader

Mahmud of Ghazni attacked India: 17 times

Mahmood Ghazni is known as the “Abductor of Scholars”.

17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in 1027 AD.

Somnath temple is located in Uttar-Pradesh of India.

questions.title: When Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation (PIDC) was established?
questions.explanation:
PIDC is a federal government entity established in 1952.

It was initially formed to develop the industrial base in Pakistan.

PIDC operates under the Ministry of Industries and Production.

It has played a key role in industrialization and economic growth.

questions.title: The Karakorum High Mountain Peaks remain covered with snow throughout the year, in this range, there are at least ______ Glaciers.
questions.explanation:
The Karakoram Range has at least 20 glaciers, making it one of the most glacier-rich regions outside the polar areas.

Notable glaciers include Siachen, Baltoro, Batura, and Hispar.

questions.title: Batura Glacier situated in ______ valley is about 57 Kilometers long.
questions.explanation:
Batura is the second largest Glacier of Pakistan.

Batura length:  64.5 km (57 km)

Batura is in Karakoram mountain ranges.

Gojal is also called Upper Hunza.

questions.title: The Britishers cruelly and inhumanly killed those who took part in the war (war of independence), 100 Indians were killed to avenge _____ British soldier/s.
questions.explanation:
After the 1857 War of Independence, the British adopted a brutal policy of repression, executing and punishing countless Indians.

In some instances, 100 Indians were killed in retaliation for the death of a single British soldier, highlighting the severity of British vengeance.

questions.title: The famous Lake “Hamoon Mashkel” is present in Baluchistan Plateau. It is the lake of _____?
questions.explanation:
Lake Hamoon Mashkel is a saltwater lake.

It is located in the Washuk District of Balochistan, Pakistan.

It is part of the Balochistan Plateau.

It is known for its saline water.

questions.title: In 1848, British entered Punjab and annexed Punjab into the British control after defeating the ______?
questions.explanation:
British defeated the Sikh Empire in the Second Anglo-Sikh War (1848–1849).

This led to the annexation of Punjab into British India in 1849.

questions.title: The first gas field Pakistan discovered in which year?
questions.explanation:
The Sui gas field is the biggest natural gas field in Pakistan.

It is located near Sui in Balochistan.

The gas field was discovered in late 1952 and the commercial exploitation of the field began in 1955.

questions.title: Animal Husbandry University is located in which city?
questions.explanation:
The University of Veterinary and Animal Sciences (UVAS), formerly known as Lahore Veterinary College,

It is a public research university located in Lahore

 Established in 1882

questions.title: Largest stadium of athletics of Pakistan was present in?
questions.explanation:
The largest stadium for athletics in Pakistan,

With a capacity of 48,700

,t is the Jinnah Sports Stadium in Islamabad.

questions.title: The Peshwa dynasty of Marathas was founded by?
questions.explanation:
The Peshwa dynasty of Marathas was founded by Balaji Vishwanath,

He was the father of Baji Rao I. 

He is considered the founder because his son, Baji Rao I, inherited the position of Peshwa after his death and expanded the Maratha empire.

questions.title: Act which allowed the Indians to compete for civil service jobs was?
questions.explanation: The Charter Act of 1853 allowed the entry of Indians into the civil services through a system of open competitive examination.

questions.title: Which former Prime Minister of Pakistan has been awarded Nishan e Pakistan on occasion of Pakistan Day 2025?
questions.explanation:
President Asif Ali Zardari posthumously awarded former prime minister Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto with Nishan-e-Pakistan in recognition of his services in the field of public service.

Awarded on 23 March 2025

His daughter Sanam Bhutto received the award.

 

Additional Information:

Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani politician & founder of Pakistan People's Party (PPP).

He became ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977.

He was executed on 4 April 1946.

****

questions.title: Pakistan is in the _____ subcontinent.
questions.explanation:
Pakistan is located in the north-western part of the South Asian subcontinent.

It shares borders with India (east), Afghanistan and Iran (west), China (north), and the Arabian Sea (south).

questions.title: Bahadur Shah Zafar was forced to live in semi-imprisonment in the gatehouse of the ______?
questions.explanation:
Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor, was exiled by the British after the 1857 War of Independence.

He was kept in semi-imprisonment in the Red Fort (Delhi) before being sent to Rangoon (Burma), where he died in 1862.

questions.title: Some ______ came to the subcontinent to raid for treasure and to make prisoners.
questions.explanation:
Invaders such as Mahmud of Ghazni, Muhammad Ghori, and the Mughals raided the Indian subcontinent for wealth, prisoners, and territorial expansion.

Their invasions led to the establishment of various dynasties and the spread of new cultures, religions, and administrative systems in the region.

questions.title: Caliph _____ demanded Raja Dahir to return his prisoners and treasure?
questions.explanation:
Caliph Al-Walid I (Umayyad Caliph) ordered Muhammad bin Qasim to lead the expedition against Raja Dahir in 712 AD.

Raja Dahir, the ruler of Sindh, refused to return Muslim prisoners and treasure, leading to the Umayyad conquest of Sindh.

questions.title: Which Pakistani won gold medal in ITF world championship Mixed Doubles category held in March 2025?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan’s tennis player Aisam-ul-Haq Qureshi and Aqeel Khan made history by winning gold in the men’s 45+ doubles category at the ITF Masters World Championship.


Additional Information: 

ITF Masters World Championship 2025 held in Manavgat, Turkey.

ITF stands for International Tennis Federation.

****

questions.title: Who among the following cricketers made fastest T20I Century for Pakistan in March 2025?
questions.explanation:
Opening batter Hasan Nawaz scored the fastest T20I century for Pakistan during the third match against New Zealand at Eden Park in Auckland on 21 March 2025.

Chasing a target of 205, Nawaz reached his maiden T20I century in just 44 balls, breaking the previous record of 49 balls held by Babar Azam, set against South Africa in 2021.

***

questions.title: Who was a Muslim commander in the state of Mysore?
questions.explanation:
Haider Ali (1721–1782) was the ruler of Mysore and a powerful Muslim military commander.

He expanded Mysore’s territory and resisted British colonial forces during his reign.

questions.title: The _______ were peaceful farmers.
questions.explanation:
The Indus Valley Civilization (c. 3300–1300 BCE) was known for its peaceful and agrarian society.

Its people engaged in farming and trade, with no significant evidence of warfare or militarization.

questions.title: The houses at Mohenjo Daro were made of ______?
questions.explanation:
The houses at Mohenjo Daro were primarily built using sun-dried and baked mud bricks.

This construction method was common in the Indus Valley Civilization due to the availability of clay and the region's hot climate.

questions.title: Pakistan is the ______ largest producer of cotton in the world.
questions.explanation:
Pakistan is the fifth-largest producer of cotton globally, following China, India, Brazil, and the United States. 

In the 2024/2025 period, Pakistan's cotton production is projected at 5 million 480-pound bales, accounting for approximately 4% of global production.

questions.title: Potwar Plateau is situated in between ______ hills in north and plain area of Punjab in south.
questions.explanation:

questions.title: Khwaja Farid Uddin was the grandfather of _______?
questions.explanation:
Khwaja Farid Uddin was the grandfather of Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

He was a prominent reformer and founder of Aligarh Movement.

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan played a key role in modern education for Muslims in British India.

questions.title: Lord Wavell convened a conference of Political Parties in June 1945 in _______?
questions.explanation:
Lord Wavell convened the Simla Conference in June 1945 to discuss India's political future.

The main issue was the representation of Muslims, but the talks failed due to disagreements between the Muslim League and Congress.

questions.title: Malwa was annexed to ______ by Allauddin Khilji in 1305.
questions.explanation:
Alauddin Khilji annexed Malwa to the Delhi Sultanate in 1305 after defeating the Paramara dynasty.

His general, Ain-ul-Mulk Multani, led the conquest and became the governor of Malwa.

questions.title: Juna Khan was the son of _______?
questions.explanation:
Juna Khan, later known as Muhammad bin Tughluq.

He was the son of Ghiyasuddin Tughluq, the founder of the Tughluq Dynasty in the Delhi Sultanate.

He became the Sultan of Delhi in 1325 after his father’s death.

questions.title: ______ Cloth Industry is one of the famous cloth industries of the world.
questions.explanation:
Faisalabad is known as the "Manchester of Pakistan" due to its large textile industry.

It is a major hub for cloth manufacturing, textile mills, and exports.

questions.title: Ghiyas Uddin ruled for only ______ years.
questions.explanation:
Ghiyas-ud-Din Tughlaq, he ruled from 1320 to 1325, which is five years.

Ghiyas-ud-Din Balban ruled the Delhi Sultanate from 1266 to 1287, a total of 21 years.

questions.title: In the history of Indo-Pak, Aryan period is that when Aryans were living in the _______ of it.
questions.explanation:
The Aryans migrated and settled in the North-Western regions of the Indian subcontinent around 1500 BCE.

This area includes present-day Pakistan and northwestern India, where the early Vedic civilization developed.

questions.title: Ayub Khan removed/deposed which politician in 1958?
questions.explanation:
On 7 October 1958 Martial law was declared by Ayub Khan.

He removed Iskander Mirza and took over the office as president of Pakistan.


Additional information

History:

President Ayub Khan resigned from office on March 25, 1969.

Ayub Khan was the commander-in-chief of the Pakistan Army.

And became the president of Pakistan after President Iskandar Ali Mirza in 1958.

He was the second president of Pakistan, serving from 1958 to 1969.

He was succeeded by General Yahya Khan.

questions.title: ______ play an important part in a country’s economy.
questions.explanation:
Exports generate revenue for a country by selling goods and services to other nations.

They contribute to economic growth, trade balance, and foreign exchange reserves.

questions.title: Sub-Himalayas are also known as ______?
questions.explanation:
Sub-Himalayas are also known as Siwalik or Outer Himalayas.

Sub-Himalayan range of the northern Indian subcontinent.

 The range spans the modern-day countries of :

  1. Pakistan,
  2. India,
  3. Nepal,
  4. and Bhutan.

questions.title: Which university bestowed honorary doctorate degree on Rahat Fateh Ali Khan?
questions.explanation:
Rahat Fateh Ali Khan received an honorary doctorate from Oxford University in 2019.
This was in recognition of his contributions to music and culture.

questions.title: The word Pakistan was firstly introduced in __________ in 1933.
questions.explanation:
The word Pakistan was firstly introduced in now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

Now or never was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: Punjab, Afghanistan, Kashmir, Sindh and Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

questions.title: The important port of Arabian Sea is ______?
questions.explanation:
Karachi is Pakistan’s largest and busiest seaport on the Arabian Sea.
It handles the majority of the country's international trade.

questions.title: Which one was the first province in Pakistan which imposed lockdown to stop the spread of Covid-19 on March 23, 2020?
questions.explanation:

questions.explanation:

Tuzuk-i-Baburi is written by Babur.

Babarnama is also known as Tuzuk-i-Baburi.

questions.explanation:
Ahmad Shah Durrani founded the first unified state in Afghanistan, the Durrani Empire, in 1747. 

 Ahmad Shah Abdali was Born in 1722, in Afghanistan.

questions.title: In which year was the educational institute of Delhi established in Indo-Pak?
questions.explanation:
The Delhi College, a significant educational institute in the Indo-Pak region, was established in 1825. 
It was closed down in 1877 and reopened in 1948. 
Other important 19th-century educational institutions in the region include:
Dar-ul-Uloom Deoband (established in 1866) 
Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (established in 1875, which later became a university in 1920).

questions.title: 2000 years old statue was found at ancient site of Mount Dillu Roy in Kot Chutta, ______ district.
questions.explanation:
• Mount Dillu Roy is an ancient archaeological site located in Dera Ghazi Khan district, Punjab, Pakistan.

  • It contains historical artifacts, including a 2000-year-old statue from the Buddhist period.
